# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

env:
  NODE_VERSION: '18.x' # Or your application's required Node.js version
  APP_BUILD_DIR: 'dist' # Or your application's build output directory

jobs:
  build_and_test:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Or your preferred runner (e.g., self-hosted for specific needs)
    timeout-minutes: 10 # Fail fast if takes too long

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm' # Cache npm dependencies

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci --prefer-offline # Use npm ci for faster, deterministic installs

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test -- --coverage --maxWorkers=50% # Run tests, potentially with coverage and optimized worker count

      - name: Build application artifacts
        run: npm run build --if-present # Only run if 'build' script exists
        env:
          CI: true # Indicate CI environment for build tools

      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: app-artifacts
          path: ${{ env.APP_BUILD_DIR }} # Path to your built application files
          retention-days: 1 # Keep artifacts for a short period to save space

  deploy_production:
    name: Deploy to Production
    needs: build_and_test # Ensures deployment only runs after build & test pass
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: production # Good practice for environment protection and visibility
      url: https://your-production-app.com # Optional: URL to your deployed application
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' # Only deploy to production from the 'main' branch
    timeout-minutes: 5 # Fail fast if deployment takes too long

    steps:
      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: app-artifacts
          path: ./${{ env.APP_BUILD_DIR }} # Download artifacts to a local directory

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials (Example for AWS deployment)
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: us-east-1 # Change to your AWS region

      - name: Deploy to S3/CloudFront (Example)
        run: |
          aws s3 sync ./${{ env.APP_BUILD_DIR }} s3://your-production-s3-bucket/ --delete
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths "/*"
        env:
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1

      # --- Alternative Deployment Examples (Uncomment/Modify as needed) ---

      # - name: Deploy to Kubernetes (Example)
      #   uses: akhilerm/kubernetes-deploy@v1.0.0 # Or specific k8s action
      #   with:
      #     kubeconfig: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_PROD }}
      #     manifests: deployment.yaml
      #     images: your-docker-registry/your-app:latest
      #   env:
      #     KUBE_NAMESPACE: production

      # - name: Deploy to Vercel (Example)
      #   run: vercel deploy --prebuilt --prod --token ${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}
      #   working-directory: ./${{ env.APP_BUILD_DIR }}

      - name: Verify deployment (Optional)
        run: |
          echo "Deployment to production successful. Access at https://your-production-app.com"
          # Add commands to ping a health check endpoint or verify content
          # curl -f https://your-production-app.com/health || { echo "Health check failed!"; exit 1; }
